reasoning: Initial observation suggests a linear relationship between position, velocity, and time. To confirm this, I will increase the time and observe the change in position.

Increasing time from 2.0 to 3.0 seconds resulted in an increase in position from 10.0 to 15.0 meters, which supports the hypothesis of a linear relationship. The next step is to increase the velocity and observe the effect on the position.

The position has increased from 15.0 meters to 30.0 meters when velocity was increased from 5.0 m/s to 10.0 m/s, keeping time constant at 3.0 seconds. This supports the hypothesis of a linear relationship. Given the initial position is 0.0, the equation should be position = velocity * time. To confirm, I will decrease the velocity and observe the change in position.
The position returned to 15.0 meters when the velocity was decreased back to 5.0 m/s, confirming the linear relationship position = velocity * time. No further action is needed to gather evidence.
